title: Treacle tart with a lattice top
subtitle: Shortcrust tart filled with golden syrup, lemon, and fresh breadcrumbs
excerpt: The classic British treacle tart: a shortcrust case filled with golden syrup mixed with lemon juice and fresh white breadcrumbs, topped with a pastry lattice. The breadcrumbs absorb the syrup and hold the filling just soft. Serves eight.
sourceNotes: Treacle tart is a British confection dating from the late nineteenth century, appearing after the introduction of refined golden syrup (Lyle's Golden Syrup was first marketed in 1883). Mrs Beeton's successor editions and Edwardian domestic cookery guides document treacle tart as a standard British pastry filled with golden syrup, lemon, and breadcrumbs. The lattice version appears in early twentieth-century British baking literature, distinguishing it from the plain open-topped form.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Best served warm on the day. The pastry softens slightly if kept overnight.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven; blind bake 15 minutes, then fill and bake 22–23 minutes until the filling is set and the lattice is deep gold

content.text: The filling is simply golden syrup, lemon, and breadcrumbs — no eggs, no cream. The breadcrumbs are the key: they absorb the syrup and give the filling body without making it solid. Use fresh white breadcrumbs rather than dried; dried crumbs produce a dense, gummy result. The pastry needs a full 30-minute chill before rolling or it will shrink during blind baking.

items.prepNote: yolk only; reserve the white for brushing

content.text: Rub the {{unsalted-butter}} into the {{plain-flour}} and {{sea-salt-fine}} until the mixture resembles coarse breadcrumbs. Add the egg yolk and 2 to 3 tablespoons of cold water; mix briefly until the dough just comes together. Flatten into a disc, wrap, and refrigerate for 30 minutes.

Preheat the oven to 190°C fan. Roll the pastry to a circle 3 mm thick and line the tart tin. Trim, leaving 1 cm overhang. Prick the base, line with paper and baking beans, and blind bake for 15 minutes. Remove the beans and paper. Mix the {{golden-syrup}}, {{lemon}} zest and juice, {{bread}} (fresh breadcrumbs), and {{double-cream}} together in a bowl. Pour into the blind-baked case. Re-roll the pastry trimmings, cut into strips, and lay in a lattice pattern across the filling. Brush the pastry with egg white. Bake for 22 to 23 minutes until the filling is barely set (it firms as it cools) and the lattice is deep gold. Cool in the tin for 20 minutes before slicing.
